revision.text:
[[az:Akiba məbədi]][[en:Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine]][[fr:Akihasan Hongū Akiha-jinja]][[ja:秋葉山本宮秋葉神社]][[zh:秋葉山本宮秋葉神社]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Akihasan Hongū Akiha Jinja
秋葉山本宮秋葉神社 | image = Akihasanhongu01.jpg | caption = Honden of Akihasan Hongū Akiha Jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.981233|137.865781|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline|qq=}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Akiba Shrines]] | deity = [[Kagu-tsuchi|Hinokagutsuchi-no-Okami]] | founded_by = | established = 701 | date_destroyed = | location = Haruno, Tenryū-ku, Hamamatsu, Shizuoka | website = {{URL|http://www.akihasanhongu.jp/|qq=}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[File:Kakegawa Gyosho Tokaido.jpg|thumb|[[Hiroshige]]]] The {{nihongo|'''Akihasan Hongū Akiha Jinja'''|秋葉山本宮秋葉神社|qq=}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in [[:en:Tenryū-ku, Hamamatsu|Tenryū-ku, Hamamatsu]] (the former town of {{ill|Haruno, Shizuoka|en|Haruno, Shizuoka|qid=Q11513827|lt=Haruno|1=Haruno, Shizuoka}} in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}). The shrine is located near the summit of {{ill|Mount Akiha|en|Mount Akiha|qid=Q501183|lt=Mount Akiha|1=Mount Akiha}}, on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Akaishi Mountains|en|Akaishi Mountains|qid=Q415350|lt=Akaishi Mountains|1=Akaishi Mountains}}. It is the head shrine of the 800 [[Akiba Shrines]]s around the country. The main festival of the shrine is held annually over three nights in December, and features ceremonies using huge flares and other fireworks.Plutschow. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. Page 173 ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Akibasan Hongū Akiba Jinja is the {{nihongo|[[Kagu-tsuchi|Hinokagutsuchi-no-Okami]]| 火之迦具土大神|qq=}}, the ''kami'' associated with protection against fires. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, this ''kami'' was popularly called the {{nihongo|'''Akiha Gongen'''|秋葉権現|qq=}} and was identified with [[:en:Kannon Bosatsu|Kannon Bosatsu]] under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu shūgō]]'' system of combined {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|qid=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}} and [[Shinto]]. ==History== Per shrine tradition, the Akibasan Hongū Akiha Jinja was established in 701 as a Buddhist temple by the famed priest {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|qid=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}}. It was named {{nihongo|Akiha-dera|秋葉寺|qq=}} from a poem written by {{ill|Emperor Saga|en|Emperor Saga|qid=Q316654|lt=Emperor Saga|1=Emperor Saga}} in 709. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} it became a center for the [[Shugendō]] cult and was associated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingon sect|1=Shingon Buddhism}}, although much of its subsequent history is uncertain. After the start of the [[:en:Tokugawa bakufu|Tokugawa bakufu]], retired ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|qid=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}}'' {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} ordered the temple to convert to the [[Sōtō|Sōtō Zen]] sect. [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]] in particular favored its blend of Shinto, Buddhism and Shugendō, and promoted the spread of the Akiha cult throughout the country to provide protection against fires. Despite its remote mountain location, the temple became a popular pilgrimage detour from the {{ill|Tōkaidō (road)|en|Tōkaidō (road)|qid=Q963514|lt=Tōkaidō|1=Tōkaidō (road)}} for pilgrims on their way to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]] or [[:en:Kompira Shrine|Kompira Shrine]], or on their way back to Edo. However, in 1685, the government banned the traditional ceremony of carrying the shrine's ''[[mikoshi]]'' along the Tōkaidō Mount Akiha towards {{ill|Edo|en|Edo|qid=Q215646|lt=Edo|1=Edo}} and {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, for fear that the riotous procession would disturb public order. The ban conversely helped spread the Akiha cult throughout the country through the establishment of numerous branch shrines, especially in the {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|qid=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} of Japan. Major Sōtō temples and monasteries often established a small Akiha shrine within their grounds. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], and the 1872 laws [[Shinbutsu bunri|separating Buddhism and Shinto]], the Shugendō cult was abolished, Buddhist images and implements were removed from the mountain to a new temple in {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|en|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|qid=Q821337|lt=Fukuroi|1=Fukuroi, Shizuoka}} and in 1873, the Akiha temple was proclaimed to be the “Akiha Shrine”. The shrine was regarded as a prefectural shrine under the {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|qid=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}} system. Most of the structures of the burned down in 1943, and were not restored until 1986. The shrine has a small museum preserving remaining artifacts, including a number of [[Japanese sword|swords]] presented as offerings by Imagawa Nakaaki, {{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|qid=Q276404|lt=Takeda Shingen|1=Takeda Shingen}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} and {{ill|Katō Kiyomasa|en|Katō Kiyomasa|qid=Q1069843|lt=Katō Kiyomasa|1=Katō Kiyomasa}}. ==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8|qq=}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Akihasan Hongū Akiha-jinja|qq=}} *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=762 Encyclopedia of Shinto] {{Reflist|qq=}} {{Shinto shrine|qq=}} {{Akiha Faith|qq=}} {{Beppyo Shrines|qq=}} [[en:Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine]] [[az:Akiba məbədi]] {{Commons category|Akihasan Hongū Akiha-jinja}} [[fr:Akihasan Hongū Akiha-jinja]] [[ja:秋葉山本宮秋葉神社]] [[zh:秋葉山本宮秋葉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q500413}} [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:天竜区の建築物]] [[Category:天竜区の歴史]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:浜松市の重要文化財]] [[Category:秋葉神社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:静岡県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]
